authoritative_sources.title: Nevada Gaming Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state gaming regulator source stating that the Commission is the final authority on licensing matters and adopts regulations governing gaming.

authoritative_sources.title: Tax & License Division
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Gaming Control Board source naming gaming licenses, compliance reviews, internal control procedures, industry variations, and monitoring responsibilities as authoritative operational records.

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Control Procedures (ICP)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Nevada Gaming Control Board, Tax & License Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official gaming workflow reference showing that slots, table games, card games, cage and credit, race and sports books, pari-mutuel, and IT all operate under explicit approved internal-control modules.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards (MICS) Version 9 for Interactive Gaming
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Nevada gaming control standard defining evidence requirements for patron registration, age and identity verification, account funding and withdrawals, excluded-person and self-exclusion controls, and interactive-gaming governance.

authoritative_sources.title: Technical Standard 6 for Interactive Gaming Systems and Associated Equipment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Nevada technical standard requiring event and exception reports, administrator-change audit trails, and systems-integrity controls for interactive-gaming systems.

authoritative_sources.title: FAQs for Indian Tribal Governments Regarding Casinos
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS gaming-industry guidance identifying when casinos become Bank Secrecy Act financial institutions and naming required AML-program, CTR, SAR, and record-retention expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AML-support prerequisite and reporting baseline intake
used_for: CTR and SAR support record expectations

authoritative_sources.title: ITG FAQ #1 Answer - What Do Suspicious Activities, BSA and FinCEN Mean to My Casino?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS workflow guidance naming suspicious activity examples, identity records that support SAR workups, and the roles of cage, finance, security, surveillance, and gaming-floor staff in AML support.

authoritative_sources.title: Alternate Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal tribal-gaming regulator guidance explaining that tribes may operate under NIGC-approved alternate standards to parts 543 and 547, which materially changes the control baseline and escalation path.

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Audit MICS Compliance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NIGC audit workflow reference tying MICS to internal-audit checklists, report expectations, and compliance review mechanics for tribal gaming operations.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and authority hierarchy intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify regulator, tribal-versus-commercial status, compact or alternate-standard posture, license class, gaming modality, and final approval owners before any compliance conclusion is produced.

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and approved-control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the relevant gaming obligations to the approved internal-control set, technical standard, and tenant policy overlays for the exact gaming area or platform under review.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and system-of-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether licensing, count and cage, surveillance, patron-account, self-exclusion, geolocation, change-control, and AML-support artifacts exist in the correct systems of record with traceable ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and review preparation
workflow_stages.description: Convert gaps, noncompliance, operational-policy conflicts, and missing approvals into remediation packets, review-ready summaries, and follow-up owners with explicit blocker logic.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary, filing, and regulator escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, suitability decisions, SAR filing calls, and final signoff while routing the issue to the correct authorized owner with preserved evidence context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Gaming license applications, renewals, findings of suitability, and work-card or registration records
Approved internal control procedures, internal control variations, and technical standard references
Count room, drop, cage, bankroll, jackpot, fills, credits, and variance logs
Surveillance approvals, surveillance review notes, incident tickets, and patron dispute or complaint records
Patron-account records, identity and age verification logs, geolocation evidence, withdrawal review artifacts, and excluded-person or self-exclusion records
AML risk assessment, employee training records, independent testing evidence, CTR support records, SAR support workpapers, and related retention controls
System change tickets, release approvals, administrator-change audit trails, and exception reports for gaming systems
Internal audit checklists, agreed-upon procedures outputs, management responses, remediation trackers, and follow-up verification evidence
systems_of_record: Regulator licensing portal or filing repository
Casino management system and player-tracking platform
AML transaction monitoring and BSA workpaper repository
Document management or GRC evidence repository
Count room, cage, or cash-management records system

allowed: Map gaming-specific obligations to approved controls, named evidence artifacts, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, audit-prep, and remediation summaries for gaming compliance workflows.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating regulator, legal, or filing authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, compact, gaming modality, license class, or approved-control version.
Conflicting regulator guidance, compact terms, approved controls, or tenant policy.
Requests that imply legal interpretation, regulator response, suitability adjudication, surveillance approval, or AML filing authority.
refuse: Any legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal licensing opinion.
Formal signoff on license, control, wagering-platform, or remediation approval.
Decisions to file or not file SARs, adjudicate patron disputes, or determine work-card or suitability outcomes.

domain_failure_modes: Treating gaming compliance as generic GRC work and failing to request regulator, compact, approved controls, or gaming modality context.
Marking readiness complete without approved internal-control evidence, count and cage records, excluded-person controls, or interactive-wagering logs.
Letting operating practice or platform convenience override approved controls, technical standards, or regulator requirements.
Failing to distinguish tribal alternate standards from commercial state rules and therefore applying the wrong control baseline.
Collapsing AML support into a filing decision or disclosing SAR-related activity.
Overgeneralizing from a casino setting and answering non-gaming hospitality or retail compliance questions as if they were gaming-lane work.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Commercial gaming regulators, tribal gaming regulatory authorities, compact terms, and NIGC-approved alternate standards do not share a single uniform control baseline across all U.S. gaming operations.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack requires runtime retrieval of jurisdiction, compact, TGRA or regulator, and approved-control version before any authoritative mapping or readiness output is produced.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Interactive-wagering control details such as geolocation evidence, self-exclusion synchronization, and patron-account controls vary by jurisdiction and platform.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack constrains outputs to evidence review against the tenant's approved platform controls and escalates when jurisdiction-specific technical standards or approvals are unavailable.
